preview_assumptions: All repo-governance targets in the activation intent are implemented exactly as declared.
Project-adapter prerequisites remain unchanged until explicitly closed.
Runtime cutover window facts remain real operating blockers and are not rewritten by activation intent alone.

facts.summary: Current CI policy skips docs-only changes, so promotion truth must treat the absence of CI evidence explicitly.
The latest local smoke evidence is failing and should remain a runtime blocker, not a core coupling point.
Repository promotion is currently unsatisfied. Treat the more specific machine facts as the real blockers instead of coupling this derived gate into activation design.
The workspace is dirty, so the loop should stop instead of promoting repo-level truth.

runtime_cutover_window.handling_rule: Resolve these for cutover timing, but do not convert them into reusable-core or repo-governance design obligations.

recommended_actions: Keep runtime blockers in a separate cutover window and do not promote them into architecture obligations.
Limit repo-governance cutover to the explicit bindings declared in the activation intent.
non_goals: Do not copy project verification commands into the reusable core.
Do not treat a dirty workspace or failing runtime evidence as reusable-core design gaps.
Do not wire active workflows before the activation boundary is explicit.
